parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces 6600+ lines across 84 files including a new retry subsystem (retry.mjs), BigModel error dispatch (bigmodel-errors.mjs), and full deletion of process.mjs. Three material risks warrant blocking: (1) silent import breakage from process.mjs removal, (2) retry-on-mutation paths that can duplicate or corrupt job state, and (3) uncoordinated concurrent writes to shared state files under multi-job workloads.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted but import graph not verified
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was fully removed. If any remaining file in scripts/ or tests/ still imports from './lib/process.mjs' or './process.mjs', the runtime will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at invocation time—not at import time in ESM dynamic-import scenarios, meaning it can surface late and mid-operation. The diff stat shows glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, job-control.mjs, and tracked-jobs.mjs all changed, making them candidates for stale imports. A single dangling import path turns the entire CLI into a brick on next launch.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/ tests/ commands/` and verify zero hits. Add a CI lint rule that flags imports of deleted modules. If any consumer remains, either restore process.mjs or migrate its callers before shipping.

findings.title: retry.mjs may re-execute non-idempotent state mutations
findings.body: retry.mjs adds 203 lines of generic retry/backoff logic. When applied to operations that mutate persisted state (job creation in tracked-jobs.mjs, state updates in state.mjs, GLM API calls in glm-client.mjs), a retry after a network timeout can produce duplicate jobs, double-written state, or conflicting status transitions if the first attempt actually succeeded at the remote. The diff shows glm-client.mjs grew ~155 lines and tracked-jobs.mjs grew ~78 lines in the same release, suggesting retry was wired into these paths. Without idempotency keys or conditional writes, retries are unsafe on state-changing calls.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site that invokes retry.withRetry (or equivalent). For each: (a) confirm the operation is read-only or idempotent, or (b) add an idempotency key / precondition check. At minimum, job-creation and state-update paths must guard against duplicate submission on retry.

findings.title: state.mjs + tracked-jobs.mjs lack write serialization
findings.body: state.mjs (32 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) both persist job metadata to the filesystem. The new --background/--wait execution modes (from review.md and job-control.mjs changes) allow multiple concurrent GLM processes. Two processes writing the same JSON state file without advisory locking can interleave partial writes, producing truncated or merged JSON that fails to parse on next read—corrupting all tracked job state silently.
findings.recommendation: Add atomic write semantics (write to temp file, then rename) to all state-file mutations. If concurrent writers are expected, add file-level advisory locking (e.g., proper-lockfile or flock). Add a corruption-recovery path that logs and resets rather than crashing.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs may swallow unexpected error shapes
findings.body: bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 new lines) dispatches error-handling based on error classification. If an unexpected error shape (missing fields, new API error code, non-standard HTTP response) falls through all match branches, the default behavior is unspecified in the diff stat. A silent fallback to 'retry' on an error that should halt (e.g., auth failure, quota exceeded) would waste retries and delay user notification by minutes.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the default/fallback branch in the error classifier logs the full error shape at warn level and classifies it as non-retryable (fail-fast) rather than retryable. Add a test case for a completely unexpected error shape to confirm it does not retry.

next_steps: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/ tests/` and report any remaining imports—this is a ship-blocker if positive.
Audit retry.mjs call sites in glm-client.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, and glm-companion.mjs for non-idempotent operations.
Verify state.mjs uses atomic writes (write+rename) for all mutations.
Add a default-case test to bigmodel-errors.test.mjs confirming unexpected errors fail-fast rather than retry.
Consider a smoke test that launches the CLI with a minimal config to confirm no ESM import errors before tagging v0.4.7.
